I've been kind of doing this Mr. Miyagi thing where we're doing a whole bunch of wax the fence, paint, yeah, and so like right now some of this stuff is kind of like how does this all fit, why are we doing this? Because it all makes more sense after dinner because now it's like all this crap we're designing, then we're plugging it into a presentation. There's a method to the madness. For example, if you look at this, when someone comes to the webinar, the first thing we're telling is our origin story. It's our first attempt to knock down the big domino. Then there's going to be things holding it up. So when we come in through and we're going to start telling our stories about the vehicle, there's going to be the one thing that's like the most important over here. So there's one that's like this is the core belief and there's a lot of other ones. And then what we're going to do is we're teaching secret number one where everyone of the webinar, we're telling the first story to kind of knock down this and then there's going to be other sub stories we tell it, knock out all these. So by the time we're done, everything in the vehicle are like crap. I have to believe that because like everyone, like these stories he's told me, like I have to now believe this is true. Then you go over here and it's like, okay, but then what's the next thing you tell the first thing and then other stories come out through here. And the same thing is by the time you go through the sequence of all of this, at that point, the big domino should have fallen and then you move into the clothes and the pitch and all that kind of stuff. So that's kind of how these things are fitting together in case anyone's thinking, I'm totally disjointed. There's a method to the madness. I promise. So, and each of these things, like as we teach this section, all these things will be knocked down, not just the one, but the one is what we're going to be focusing on because that's we have to have something sexy to call this to get people excited as we dive in. The next thing we're going to be doing now is hopefully you did the exercise. If not, this next thing is going to be really hard for you. So you have each of these things. The number one false belief they have is this, this is the false belief, this is the false belief. So I'm going to take this now and what we're going to do is we are going to title that and that something. And so we've got to have a sexy name to get people all excited and interested. 90% of this business you guys is curiosity, getting people like, what is that thing? Okay, every time someone's got a webinar doesn't convert, like most of the time it's like, there's not a curiosity to get people excited. Like, I think people get webinar, they'll do a webinar, they'll get a thousand people to register in like 200 or like, 20 people show up and it's because there's not a curiosity. They hear about it and like, oh, I can figure it. They look at that and they're like, I think I know the answer to that, right? So the more curiosity you have where they can't quite figure it out, that's what gets people to show up. That gets them like, okay, I got to put this on my calendar because there's something there that I do not know, I can't figure it out by myself, right? So what we're going to do is take these three core false beliefs that you have right now and knowing the epiphany bridge story that I have associated with this. So here's my epiphany bridge story for each of these. What is a really interesting, unique, sexy, crazy, curiosity-based title that's going to get people like, dang, I have to be on this webinar. So that's going to be the epiphany bridge story that I'm explaining to them that's going to knock down this false belief. That's the way that I kind of positioned it, right? So the false belief that they would have here is basically that I have to go create a funnel. Then when I started the supplement company and I just copied someone, I modeled it into work and then it worked and then I had, I showed Jameil and Drew and then they did it and so I'm just kind of going to the store and something like, oh cool, like I don't have to think through anything. I just got to find something that worked and started to have success and we funnel hack and we model it, boom, then I believe, like then this thing falls down. He's like, they see the price and first like 30 grand. And then when you have from that point forward, the rest of the presentation is I got to prove that it's worth 30 grand. It doesn't make any sense. How can I ethically steal something? Like it doesn't make, like you have to be on to get the answer. So the whole like, um, my first mentor, Mark Joyner, and he's not on episode of the funnel hacker TV, he has a history, but, um, he had this concept. He used to call it a Zergarnik effect. They never heard that before. Um, and the story behind it says it was a study done in like the sixties where they, uh, they found out that like a waiter or waitress would go and they would take an order and not even have to write down and they'd remember perfectly. And then they'd go and they'd come, they get the order and then two seconds later, they have completely forgotten what the thing was. But until they completed that loop, they can remember everything with like, have you ever had that waitresses that like, they know everything? You're like, how did you remember that? And then they don't forget to like, cause like, until the loops closed, like they can't forget it and our brains work that way. And so like it's all in NLP. They call it like opening and closing loops. Like you open a loop where it's like, what's the, and they drag to the next thing reality shows do that every time it's about to end. They open some loop that like freaks you out and you have to watch the commercials to see the next thing soap operas do. It's like, all these things do this. So it's like same thing. I'm trying to open these curiosity loops. We're like, I can't solve that unless I'm on the webinar. Like, go, people are always like, no one shows my webinar. What do I do? I'm like, well, you're not like creating enough rock curiosity. We're like, I've had some webinars like I'm a registered. I'll watch the replay if it comes out and not at school. One of the best sales pitches at all time is the dead doctors don't lie. Anyone ever heard that presentation? Like I've studied that presentation like crazy, but like his whole pitch is like, he's a veterinarian. He's like, do you guys know that we can actually cure cancer and AIDS and all these things in pigs and horses and goats and stuff, but is it is illegal for you to cure a disease in America? Illegal. Yeah. We can do it in animals. So let me show you the animals because he's a vet and he's like, he bridges this, makes this bridge. We're just like, dang, that cures animals. Like I'm not legally allowed to say cures humans, but it cures pigs. So it's like one of the greatest pitches of all time. Like it's, it's amazing. Dead doctors don't lie. So when I was like 12 year old Russell ordering junk mail all the time, I used to get like 20 tapes a week in the mail that said, dead doctors don't lie. I used to listen to that over and I was like, go like, this is the cool, like I was freaking out, right? It was Dr. Walik's pitch way back then. And then fast forward 20 years later, he actually came to Boise one time. I was like, oh, I'm like freaking out. So I went to the thing I'm sitting there, like front row, like a little nerd, like this is Dr. Walik and listening to his things. And it was just like, ah, such a good pitch. He was definitely saying things illegal throughout the whole thing, but he did a good job of like bridging these things and sold me a whole bunch of product, which is awesome. So I love being sold. All right. Okay. So this is next, you know, just so you guys know, see us some, uh, we did this in like 10, 15 minutes. So, um, typically for me to figure the one thing in the three secrets out, I'll spend at least a day in front of a whiteboard. Like it's not always just like, oh, there's the answer. Like when we did one for the network marketing thing, it was me, John, Steven, and we're sitting in front of a whiteboard, literally for an entire day. So I'm like, God, no, that's not right. It's not sexy. It's not exciting. We kept going over and it till finally like we got it. And it was just like, yes, but it took, sometimes it takes a while. So don't stress that if I didn't get it fast, like I don't usually get it fast. It takes a while to get it right. I just want to get the wheel spinning and then go understanding that. So as you go back to create the final versions and stuff, um, just know, it's only takes a while to get the right thing. But when you have the right thing, like it's, it's, it's amazing. Okay. All right. So we're under it now. We're going to do some fun is tomorrow when we start building out some of these, the actual funnels for this, uh, one of the big things, uh, when somebody registers from webinars, the time of the register for webinar, and there's a time when they actually show up to it and there's this, this gap in between sometimes. And so what we try to do is create like an indoctrination sequence is what I usually call it. Uh, Garrett White called it, uh, what do you call these? Presiduction, uh, presiduction sequence to presiduce them. So when they show up, they're ready. Um, it doesn't really matter. But what we're typically doing during our presiduction stuff is we're teasing these things. So we get people excited. So like they made a register for the webinar. They saw the thing like my weird niche funnel. It's currently making me 17th at whatever per day and how to knock it off less than 10 minutes. So that's what they see. They register. They're all excited. And then they webinar for two days or not for a day, whatever that is. Then I'm sending out emails where first email is like, Hey, I'm going to show you guys how to ethically silver a million dollars with the funnel hacks from your competitors for under a hundred bucks. Check out this video. So I explain how it works. And my video of me talking about this, I'm not going to give the answer because then they're not going to show up the webinar. I'm going to talk about this and get them all fired up and freaked out. So they're like, God, I got to watch this webinar. Like that sounds so, so, so amazing. Right. And then video day number two, I'm in a video that talks about secret number two and day three of the video about secret number three. So it's just me teasing them about whatever this, this thing is going to be.
